title: Depth-Series Forward Model
text: depth-series model needs convert sample depth (measure lab) sample age (drives racemization). simplest case constant sedimentation rate: real core radiocarbon-dated horizons, supply tie-points directly:
code: # 50 cm/ka sedimentation rate; 10 ka core = 500 cm am <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500)  # Verify the round-trip am$depth_to_age(250)  # should be 5 ka #> [1] 5 am$age_to_depth(5)    # should be 250 cm #> [1] 250 am_real <- make_age_model(   depth_cm = c(  0,  45, 120, 280, 420),   age_ka   = c(  0,   1,   3,   7,  10) )  depths <- seq(0, 420, by = 5) plot(am_real$depth_to_age(depths), depths, type = "l",      xlab = "Age (ka BP)", ylab = "Depth (cm)", main = "Piecewise age-depth model",      ylim = rev(range(depths)))

what: Sanity check: constant temperature, constant rate
title: Depth-Series Forward Model
text: constant sedimentation rate constant bottom water temperature, D/L increase monotonically depth (older = racemized) age:   shape D/L-vs-age curve reflects power-law kinetics: fastest accumulation D/L low (fresh sediment), slowing progressively D/L rises.
code: am     <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500) depths <- seq(0, 500, by = 10) out    <- racemize_depth_series(depths, age_model = am, temp_model = 5)  ggplot(out, aes(x = DL, y = depth_cm)) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_reverse() +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)", title = "D/L vs. depth") +   theme_bw() ggplot(out, aes(x = deposition_age_ka, y = DL)) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   labs(x = "Deposition age (ka BP)", y = "D/L", title = "D/L vs. age") +   theme_bw()

title: Depth-Series Forward Model
text: Higher sedimentation rates spread age range depth, shifting D/L profile greater depths. Comparing three rates bottom water temperature:  high sedimentation rates, core samples shorter time period given depth, D/L values lower throughout.
code: rates  <- c(25, 50, 100)  # cm/ka depths <- seq(0, 500, by = 10)  res <- lapply(rates, function(r) {   am  <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = r, max_depth_cm = 500)   out <- racemize_depth_series(depths, age_model = am, temp_model = 5)   out$rate_cm_ka <- r   out })  do.call(rbind, res) |>   mutate(rate_label = paste(rate_cm_ka, "cm/ka")) |>   ggplot(aes(x = DL, y = depth_cm, color = rate_label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_reverse() +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)", color = "Sed. rate",        title = "Effect of sedimentation rate on D/L profile (T = 5°C)") +   theme_bw()

title: Depth-Series Forward Model
text: seasonal mode applies thermal diffusion attenuation summer/winter anomaly. Samples shallow depths experience stronger seasonal forcing; deeper (older) samples converge mean annual temperature. Arrhenius nonlinearity means seasonality always increases D/L relative mean-annual case mean temperature.  Note gap narrows depth: ~300 cm (~6 ka 50 cm/ka), annual diffusion e-folding depth (~0.71 m κ\kappa = 1.6 m²/yr) attenuated seasonal signal substantially, curves converge.
code: am     <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500) depths <- seq(0, 500, by = 5)  # Mean-annual mode: constant 5°C out_ann <- racemize_depth_series(depths, age_model = am, temp_model = 5)  # Seasonal mode: same mean T but ±5°C seasonality out_sea <- racemize_depth_series(depths,                                  age_model  = am,                                  temp_model = 5,                                  sumT_model = 10,                                  winT_model = 0)  bind_rows(   mutate(out_ann, mode = "mean-annual (5°C)"),   mutate(out_sea, mode = "seasonal (0–10°C, mean=5°C)") ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = DL, y = depth_cm, color = mode)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_reverse() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("steelblue", "tomato")) +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)", color = NULL,        title = "Mean-annual vs. seasonal mode",        subtitle = "Seasonal D/L > mean-annual at same mean T (Jensen's inequality)") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Depth-Series Forward Model
text: Holocene cooling scenario, pass data frame temp_model:  cooling scenario produces higher D/L depth (older samples experienced warmer temperatures early history), even though profiles time-averaged temperature. signal inversion framework designed detect. ```
code: am     <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500) depths <- seq(0, 500, by = 10)  # Linear cooling from 8°C at 10 ka to 5°C at present cooling_hist <- data.frame(age_ka = c(0, 10), temp_C = c(5, 8))  out_cool <- racemize_depth_series(depths, age_model = am,                                    temp_model = cooling_hist) out_flat <- racemize_depth_series(depths, age_model = am,                                    temp_model = 6.5)  # same mean  bind_rows(   mutate(out_cool, scenario = "Holocene cooling (8→5°C)"),   mutate(out_flat, scenario = "Constant 6.5°C (same mean)") ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = DL, y = depth_cm, color = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_y_reverse() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("steelblue", "tomato")) +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)", color = NULL,        title = "Effect of Holocene temperature trend on D/L profile") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Racemization Fundamentals
text: Amino acid racemization temperature-dependent chemical reaction. rate constant kk temperature TT (Kelvin) follows Arrhenius equation: ln⁡(k)=−EaRT−1+ln⁡(Ae)\ln(k) = -\frac{E_a}{R} T^{-1} + \ln(A_e) arrhenius() function implements literature defaults (Ae=e42.12A_e = e^{42.12}, Ea=31.5E_a = 31.5 kcal/mol):  exponential dependence 1/T1/T means small temperature differences large effects racemization rate — 5°C warming roughly doubles kk near lake bottom-water temperatures.
code: T_C  <- seq(-5, 25, by = 1) kt   <- arrhenius(Temp = T_C + 273)  plot(T_C, kt, type = "l", xlab = "Temperature (°C)", ylab = "Rate constant k",      main = "Arrhenius rate constant for isoleucine epimerization")

title: Racemization Fundamentals
text: Racemization integrated (D/L)x(D/L)^x space rather D/LD/L space (Bada linearization). converts sigmoidal D/L accumulation curve linear one, making relationship D/Lx=kTt+CD/L^x = k_T t + C tractable:
code: dT  <- 10         # 10-year timestep age <- seq(0, 400, by = dT) / 1000  # ka  # Accumulate racemization at constant 5°C kt  <- arrhenius(Temp = 5 + 273) Rx  <- cumsum(c(0, rep(dRacPL(kt, dT), length(age) - 1)))  data.frame(age_ka = age, Rx = Rx, DL = Rx^(1/3)) |>   (\(d) {     par(mfrow = c(1, 2))     plot(d$age_ka, d$Rx,  type = "l", xlab = "Age (ka)", ylab = expression((D/L)^x),          main = "Linear in power-law space")     plot(d$age_ka, d$DL,  type = "l", xlab = "Age (ka)", ylab = "D/L",          main = "Sigmoidal in D/L space")     par(mfrow = c(1, 1))   })()

title: Racemization Fundamentals
text: Arrhenius relationship nonlinear (exponential 1/T1/T), two climates mean annual temperature different seasonal amplitudes accumulate different D/L values. Warmer summers contribute disproportionately cooler winters subtract (Jensen’s inequality).  400 ka, high-seasonality scenario (sum=19/win=0) accumulated noticeably racemization low-seasonality scenario (sum=11/win=8), even though mean annual temperature identical. model treats summer winter half-years separately. course ’re looking lake sediments, ’s hard get much seasonality water, especially accumulating sediment damp variability push everything towards mean annual temperature.
code: dT  <- 1000 age <- seq(0, 400000, by = dT) / 1000  # Three scenarios with the same mean annual T = 9.5°C scenarios <- list(   list(sumT = 15, winT =  4, label = "sum=15, win=4 (mean=9.5)"),   list(sumT = 19, winT =  0, label = "sum=19, win=0 (mean=9.5)"),   list(sumT = 11, winT =  8, label = "sum=11, win=8 (mean=9.5)") )  res <- lapply(scenarios, function(s) {   Rx <- 0   for (i in 2:length(age)) {     Rx[i] <- Rx[i-1] +       dRacPL(arrhenius(Temp = s$sumT + 273), dT / 2) +       dRacPL(arrhenius(Temp = s$winT + 273), dT / 2)   }   data.frame(age_ka = age, DL = Rx^(1/3), seasonality = s$label) })  do.call(rbind, res) |>   ggplot(aes(x = age_ka, y = DL, color = seasonality)) +   geom_line() +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   labs(x = "Age (ka)", y = "D/L", color = NULL,        title = "Same mean annual T, different seasonality → different D/L") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Racemization Fundamentals
text: well can D/L distinguish different Holocene temperature histories? compare cooling, stable, warming summer temperature trends 10 ka winter temperature held constant 4°C:  three scenarios mean annual temperature interval, yet D/L profiles diverge. cooling scenario (warm early Holocene) accumulates D/L faster first, producing higher final value warming scenario. forward-model sensitivity Bayesian inversion framework designed exploit. ```
code: dT  <- 10 age <- seq(0, 10000, by = dT) / 1000 winT <- rep(4, length(age))  trends <- list(   list(sumT = seq(15, 10, length.out = length(age)), label = "cooling"),   list(sumT = rep(12.5,   length(age)),              label = "stable"),   list(sumT = seq(10, 15, length.out = length(age)), label = "warming") )  res <- lapply(trends, function(tr) {   Rx <- 0   for (i in 2:length(age)) {     Rx[i] <- Rx[i-1] +       dRacPL(arrhenius(Temp = tr$sumT[i] + 273), dT / 2) +       dRacPL(arrhenius(Temp = winT[i]    + 273), dT / 2)   }   data.frame(age_ka = age, DL = Rx^(1/3), scenario = tr$label) })  do.call(rbind, res) |>   ggplot(aes(x = age_ka, y = DL, color = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   labs(x = "Age (ka BP)", y = "D/L",        title = "Holocene temperature trends and D/L accumulation") +   theme_bw()

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: Stage 1 estimates three AAR kinetic parameters (AeA_e: pre-exponential factor; EaE_a: activation energy; xx: power-law exponent) two data sources: Lab heating experiments: surface sediment heated 80, 100, 110°C known durations. Temperature controlled; time controlled; resulting D/L measured. data tightly constrain Arrhenius relationship. Downcore data 4°C lakes: lakes whose hypolimnion stays near-constant 4°C year-round (deep, cold-climate lakes). temperature effectively known, downcore D/L profile constrains D/L accumulates time (.e., power-law exponent xx). Together, two data types break degeneracy among three parameters. work transformed space: log⁡(Ae)\log(A_e) (since Ae≈e42A_e \approx e^{42} numerically unwieldy) log⁡(σ)\log(\sigma) observation noise (enforces positivity without hard truncation).

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: prototype use synthetic data known true parameters can verify recovery.
code: set.seed(42) true_AAR <- default_AAR_params  # Ae=exp(42.12), Ea=31.5, x=3  # Heating experiments: 3 temps × 8 times × 3 replicates = 72 observations heating_data <- sim_heating_data(AAR_params = true_AAR, sigma = 0.01, seed = 1)  # Calibration core: 30 samples at 500-yr spacing to 15 ka in a 4°C lake am_cal   <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 750) cal_data <- sim_downcore_data(am_cal,                               temp_model     = 4,                               sample_ages_ka = seq(0.5, 15, by = 0.5),                               AAR_params     = true_AAR,                               sigma          = 0.015,                               seed           = 2)

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
code: # Heating experiments ggplot(heating_data, aes(x = time_yr, y = DL_obs,                           color = factor(temp_C), group = factor(temp_C))) +   geom_point(size = 1.5) +   geom_line(aes(y = DL_pred), linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_x_log10() +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   labs(x = "Heating duration (yr, log scale)", y = "D/L",        color = "Temp (°C)", title = "Synthetic heating experiment data") +   theme_bw() # Calibration core ggplot(cal_data, aes(x = DL_obs, y = depth_cm)) +   geom_point(size = 1.5, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(x = DL_pred), colour = "grey50") +   scale_y_reverse() +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)",        title = "Synthetic 4°C calibration core") +   theme_bw()

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: log-posterior combines two likelihoods (heating experiments calibration core) prior four sampled parameters. Inspecting priors running sampler confirms broad relative data observations drive posterior. Centres drawn published Ile-Ile estimates (Kaufman 2000). Standard deviations wide enough allow data move posterior substantially, still ruling physically implausible values.
code: prior_pars <- list(   log_Ae    = c(mean = 42.12,     sd = 2),   Ea        = c(mean = 31.5,      sd = 5),   x         = c(mean = 3,         sd = 0.5),   log_sigma = c(mean = log(0.02), sd = 1) )  prior_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(prior_pars), function(nm) {   p <- prior_pars[[nm]]   v <- seq(p["mean"] - 3.5 * p["sd"], p["mean"] + 3.5 * p["sd"],            length.out = 400)   data.frame(parameter = nm, value = v,              density   = dnorm(v, p["mean"], p["sd"])) }))  ggplot(prior_df, aes(x = value, y = density)) +   geom_area(fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "darkorange", linewidth = 0.9) +   facet_wrap(~parameter, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +   labs(x = "Value", y = "Prior density",        title = "Stage 1 priors: kinetic parameters") +   theme_bw()

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: run random-walk Metropolis-Hastings chain. Proposal standard deviations set target roughly 25% acceptance rate. Check acceptance_rate output adjust prop_kin needed.
code: init_kin <- c(log_Ae    = 42.12,               Ea        = 31.5,               x         = 3.0,               log_sigma = log(0.01))  prop_kin <- c(log_Ae    = 0.005,               Ea        = 0.05,               x         = 0.003,               log_sigma = 0.008) / 2  mcmc_kin <- run_mcmc(log_posterior_kinetics,                      init        = init_kin,                      n_iter      = 10000,                      proposal_sd = prop_kin,                      heating_data      = heating_data,                      downcore_cal_data = cal_data,                      age_model_cal     = am_cal,                      temp_cal          = 4)  cat("Acceptance rate:", round(mcmc_kin$acceptance_rate * 100, 1), "%\n") #> Acceptance rate: 25.9 %

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: Burn-(grey) discarded summarising posterior. Chains look like white noise burn-, trend drift.
code: plot_mcmc_chains(mcmc_kin, burnin = 1000)

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: Red dashed lines show true parameter values used generate data. Orange curves show prior density. posterior (blue fill) much narrower prior, confirming data informative, centred near true values.
code: true_kin <- c(log_Ae    = 42.12,               Ea        = 31.5,               x         = 3.0,               log_sigma = log(0.01))  plot_posterior_kinetics(mcmc_kin, true_params = true_kin, burnin = 1000) +   geom_line(data = prior_df, aes(x = value, y = density),             colour = "darkorange", linewidth = 0.9, inherit.aes = FALSE)

title: Bayesian Kinetics Estimation (Stage 1)
text: Draw 200 parameter sets posterior run forward model confirm fitted parameters reproduce calibration core observations:  Points synthetic observations; shaded ribbon 95% posterior predictive interval. Good coverage points confirms model fits data. kinetics estimates passed Stage 2. ```
code: depths_check <- seq(0, 750, by = 15) posterior_predictive_DL(mcmc_kin,                         age_model     = am_cal,                         depths_cm     = depths_check,                         temp_model    = 4,                         observed_data = cal_data,                         burnin        = 1000,                         n_draws       = 200)

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: Stage 2 takes kinetic parameters estimated Stage 1 fixed infers temperature history new (“target”) core whose thermal history unknown. unknown parameterised vector temperatures 6 control knots (0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 ka BP) connected linear interpolation. first-difference smoothness prior penalises large step--step changes. two-stage approach avoids simultaneously inferring kinetics temperature data, highly -determined.

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: regenerate Stage 1 MCMC fixed seed kinetics reproducible across vignettes: Extract posterior median kinetic parameters use fixed values Stage 2:
code: true_AAR <- default_AAR_params  # Heating experiments heating_data <- sim_heating_data(AAR_params = true_AAR, sigma = 0.01, seed = 1)  # Calibration core (4°C lake) am_cal   <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 750) cal_data <- sim_downcore_data(am_cal,                               temp_model     = 4,                               sample_ages_ka = seq(0.5, 15, by = 0.5),                               AAR_params     = true_AAR,                               sigma          = 0.015,                               seed           = 2)  init_kin <- c(log_Ae = 42.12, Ea = 31.5, x = 3.0, log_sigma = log(0.01)) prop_kin <- c(log_Ae = 0.05,  Ea = 0.3,  x = 0.05, log_sigma = 0.1) / 5  mcmc_kin <- run_mcmc(log_posterior_kinetics,                      init              = init_kin,                      n_iter            = 10000,                      proposal_sd       = prop_kin,                      heating_data      = heating_data,                      downcore_cal_data = cal_data,                      age_model_cal     = am_cal,                      temp_cal          = 4)  cat("Stage 1 acceptance rate:", round(mcmc_kin$acceptance_rate * 100, 1), "%\n") #> Stage 1 acceptance rate: 10.4 % burnin_idx   <- 1001:10000 kin_post     <- mcmc_kin$samples[burnin_idx, ]  kin_fixed <- c(   log_Ae    = median(kin_post[, "log_Ae"]),   Ea        = median(kin_post[, "Ea"]),   x         = median(kin_post[, "x"]),   log_sigma = median(kin_post[, "log_sigma"]) )  cat("Fixed kinetics used in Stage 2:\n") #> Fixed kinetics used in Stage 2: print(round(kin_fixed, 4)) #>    log_Ae        Ea         x log_sigma  #>   42.3394   31.6627    3.0359   -4.3888

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: target core comes lake unknown temperature history. demonstration use linear Holocene cooling scenario: 8°C 10 ka BP declining 5°C present. Samples span 0.5 10 ka 500-yr spacing.
code: true_T_history <- data.frame(age_ka = c(0, 10), temp_C = c(5, 8))  am_target    <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 40, max_depth_cm = 400) target_data  <- sim_downcore_data(am_target,                                    temp_model     = true_T_history,                                    sample_ages_ka = seq(0.5, 10, by = 0.5),                                    AAR_params     = true_AAR,                                    sigma          = 0.015,                                    seed           = 3)  ggplot(target_data, aes(x = DL_obs, y = depth_cm)) +   geom_point(size = 1.5, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_line(aes(x = DL_pred), colour = "grey50") +   scale_y_reverse() +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)",        title = "Synthetic target core (true cooling: 8°C → 5°C)") +   theme_bw()

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: Stage 2 three prior components temperature history parameters: Marginal temperature prior — knot temperature receives weakly informative Normal(5, 3) prior, centred near present-day Arctic/sub-Arctic mean annual temperatures. ±3σ range spans roughly −4°C 14°C, easily encompasses Holocene variability without constraining inference particular trend. Smoothness prior — adjacent knot differences receive Normal(0, 2) penalty log scale. encodes physical expectation gradual Holocene temperature change: 2-ka step sizes larger ~4°C (2σ) strongly penalised impossible. Noise prior — log(σ) receives Normal(log(0.02), 1), Stage 1, centred near HPLC analytical precision.  Drawing 200 independent trajectories marginal knot prior gives prior predictive envelope — range temperature histories considered plausible D/L data observed:
code: knot_ages <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10)  # ka BP # Marginal temperature prior and smoothness prior prior_T_df <- rbind(   data.frame(     component     = "Knot temperature:  N(5, 3)",     value         = seq(-4, 14, length.out = 300),     density       = dnorm(seq(-4, 14, length.out = 300), 5, 3)   ),   data.frame(     component     = "Step size (2-ka):  N(0, 2)",     value         = seq(-7, 7, length.out = 300),     density       = dnorm(seq(-7, 7, length.out = 300), 0, 2)   ) )  ggplot(prior_T_df, aes(x = value, y = density)) +   geom_area(fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "darkorange", linewidth = 0.9) +   facet_wrap(~component, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +   labs(x = "Value (°C)", y = "Prior density",        title = "Stage 2 priors: temperature history") +   theme_bw() set.seed(7) n_prior     <- 200 prior_T_mat <- matrix(rnorm(n_prior * length(knot_ages), mean = 5, sd = 3),                       nrow = n_prior)  summ_prior <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(knot_ages), function(j) {   data.frame(     age_ka = knot_ages[j],     lo95   = quantile(prior_T_mat[, j], 0.025),     hi95   = quantile(prior_T_mat[, j], 0.975),     lo50   = quantile(prior_T_mat[, j], 0.25),     hi50   = quantile(prior_T_mat[, j], 0.75)   ) }))  ggplot(summ_prior, aes(x = age_ka)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95),               fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.2) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo50, ymax = hi50),               fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.4) +   scale_x_reverse() +   labs(x = "Age (ka BP)", y = "Temperature (°C)",        title = "Prior predictive envelope: temperature history",        subtitle = "Inner band: 50% prior interval; outer band: 95% prior interval") +   theme_bw()

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: temperature parameterisation uses 6 control knots. Initialise near plausible flat temperature use moderately diffuse proposal:
code: init_T <- c(T_0ka  = 6, T_2ka  = 6, T_4ka  = 6,             T_6ka  = 6, T_8ka  = 6, T_10ka = 6,             log_sigma = log(0.015))  prop_T <- c(T_0ka  = 0.3, T_2ka  = 0.3, T_4ka  = 0.3,             T_6ka  = 0.3, T_8ka  = 0.3, T_10ka = 0.3,             log_sigma = 0.1) * 2  mcmc_T <- run_mcmc(log_posterior_temperature,                    init              = init_T,                    n_iter            = 10000,                    proposal_sd       = prop_T,                    knot_ages_ka      = knot_ages,                    kinetics_params   = kin_fixed,                    downcore_target_data = target_data,                    age_model_target  = am_target)  cat("Stage 2 acceptance rate:", round(mcmc_T$acceptance_rate * 100, 1), "%\n") #> Stage 2 acceptance rate: 39.3 %

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: Check chains mix well across temperature knots:
code: plot_mcmc_chains(mcmc_T, burnin = 1000)

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: reconstruction shows posterior median (line), 95% credible interval (blue ribbon), true temperature history (red dashed line), 95% prior predictive interval (orange ribbon). posterior substantially narrower prior, demonstrating D/L data informative temperature:  true cooling trend (red dashed line) fall within credible envelope. Uncertainty largest oldest end record, cumulative integration noisy D/L data limits resolution. contrast wide orange prior narrow blue posterior quantifies much information D/L profile contributes.
code: true_T_df <- data.frame(age_ka = knot_ages,                         temp_C = c(5, 5.6, 6.2, 6.8, 7.4, 8))  plot_temperature_reconstruction(mcmc_T,                                 knot_ages_ka   = knot_ages,                                 true_T_history = true_T_df,                                 burnin         = 1000) +   geom_ribbon(data = summ_prior,               aes(x = age_ka, ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95),               fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.15, inherit.aes = FALSE) +   labs(subtitle = "Orange: 95% prior; blue: 95% posterior")

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: Verify recovered temperature history reproduces observed D/L profile. Stage 2 draw temperature knots posterior, build temperature history per draw, run forward model fixed kinetics:
code: post_T  <- tail(mcmc_T$samples, nrow(mcmc_T$samples) - 1000) T_cols  <- grep("^T_", colnames(post_T), value = TRUE) idx     <- sample(nrow(post_T), 200)  kin_AAR <- list(Ae = exp(kin_fixed[["log_Ae"]]),                 Ea = kin_fixed[["Ea"]],                 R  = 0.001987,                 x  = kin_fixed[["x"]])  depths_check <- seq(0, 400, by = 10)  pred_list <- lapply(idx, function(i) {   tmp_model <- data.frame(age_ka = knot_ages,                           temp_C = as.numeric(post_T[i, T_cols]))   fwd       <- racemize_depth_series(depths_check, am_target, tmp_model,                                      AAR_params = kin_AAR)   fwd$draw  <- i   fwd }) pred_df <- do.call(rbind, pred_list)  summ <- pred_df |>   dplyr::group_by(depth_cm) |>   dplyr::summarise(median = median(DL),                    lo95   = quantile(DL, 0.025),                    hi95   = quantile(DL, 0.975),                    .groups = "drop")  ggplot(summ, aes(x = median, y = depth_cm)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(xmin = lo95, xmax = hi95, y = depth_cm),               fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(data = target_data, aes(x = DL_obs, y = depth_cm),              colour = "black", size = 1.5) +   scale_y_reverse() +   labs(x = "D/L", y = "Depth (cm)",        title = "Posterior predictive D/L profile") +   theme_bw()

title: Holocene Temperature Reconstruction (Stage 2)
text: Several factors govern width credible interval: Number samples: observations reduce uncertainty. used 20 samples. Observation noise (sigma): analytical precision D/L measurement. used σ≈0.01\sigma \approx 0.01 0.020.02, realistic? Kinetics uncertainty: fixed kinetics Stage 1 posterior median. Propagating full Stage 1 posterior (.e., drawing kinetics temperature jointly) widen credible interval . , turn proper Bayesian Hierarchical Model. ```

title: Authors and Citation
text: Nick McKay. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: McKay N (2026). AARP: Forward Model Bayesian Inversion Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry. R package version 0.1.0, https://nickmckay.github.io/AARP.
code: @Manual{,   title = {AARP: Forward Model and Bayesian Inversion for Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry},   author = {Nick McKay},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://nickmckay.github.io/AARP}, }

title: AARP — Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry
text: Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry — forward model Bayesian inversion framework inferring Holocene paleotemperatures D/L ratios isoleucine lake sediment. Amino acid racemization (AAR) temperature-sensitive: rate L-enantiomer converts D-form depends time temperature via Arrhenius kinetics. sediment core whose age–depth relationship independently constrained (e.g. radiocarbon), downcore D/L profile encodes integrated thermal history lake bottom. AARP provides machinery decode .

title: AARP — Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("nickmckay/AARP")

title: AARP — Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry
text: Simultaneous inference kinetics temperature single core -determined. AARP separates problem two stages: Stage 1 uses heating experiments constrain Arrhenius relationship known-temperature (4 °C hypolimnion) calibration core pin power-law exponent. Stage 2 fixes kinetics Stage 1 posterior median inverts D/L new core temperature.
code: Stage 1 — Kinetics calibration   ├── Lab heating experiments (80/100/110 °C, controlled time)   └── Downcore D/L from a 4 °C lake (known temperature)           ↓   Posterior on (log Ae, Ea, x, log σ)  Stage 2 — Temperature reconstruction   ├── Fixed kinetics from Stage 1   └── Downcore D/L from target lake (unknown temperature)           ↓   Posterior on temperature history T(t) at 6 control knots

title: AARP — Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry
code: library(AARP)  # Build a constant-rate age–depth model (50 cm/ka, 750 cm core) am <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 750)  # Simulate a downcore D/L profile under a linear 5→8 °C Holocene cooling T_history <- data.frame(age_ka = c(0, 10), temp_C = c(5, 8)) dl <- racemize_depth_series(seq(0, 750, by = 25), am, T_history,                             AAR_params = default_AAR_params) head(dl)

title: AARP — Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry
code: set.seed(1) heating_data <- sim_heating_data(sigma = 0.01, seed = 1) cal_data     <- sim_downcore_data(am, temp_model = 4,                                   sample_ages_ka = seq(0.5, 15, by = 0.5),                                   sigma = 0.015, seed = 2)  mcmc_kin <- run_mcmc(   log_posterior_kinetics,   init        = c(log_Ae = 42.12, Ea = 31.5, x = 3.0, log_sigma = log(0.01)),   n_iter      = 10000,   proposal_sd = c(log_Ae = 0.005, Ea = 0.05, x = 0.003, log_sigma = 0.008),   heating_data      = heating_data,   downcore_cal_data = cal_data,   age_model_cal     = am,   temp_cal          = 4 )  plot_mcmc_chains(mcmc_kin, burnin = 1000) plot_posterior_kinetics(mcmc_kin, burnin = 1000)

title: AARP — Amino Acid Racemization Paleothermometry
code: kin_fixed <- apply(mcmc_kin$samples[1001:10000, ], 2, median)  target_data <- sim_downcore_data(am, temp_model = T_history,                                  sample_ages_ka = seq(0.5, 10, by = 0.5),                                  sigma = 0.015, seed = 3)  knot_ages <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10)  mcmc_T <- run_mcmc(   log_posterior_temperature,   init        = c(T_0ka = 6, T_2ka = 6, T_4ka = 6,                   T_6ka = 6, T_8ka = 6, T_10ka = 6, log_sigma = log(0.015)),   n_iter      = 10000,   proposal_sd = c(T_0ka = 0.3, T_2ka = 0.3, T_4ka = 0.3,                   T_6ka = 0.3, T_8ka = 0.3, T_10ka = 0.3, log_sigma = 0.1),   knot_ages_ka         = knot_ages,   kinetics_params      = kin_fixed,   downcore_target_data = target_data,   age_model_target     = am )  plot_temperature_reconstruction(mcmc_T, knot_ages_ka = knot_ages, burnin = 1000)

what: Arrhenius rate constant for amino acid racemization — arrhenius
title: Arrhenius rate constant for amino acid racemization — arrhenius
text: Computes temperature-dependent rate constant \(k\) Arrhenius equation: $$\ln(k) = -\frac{E_a}{R} T^{-1} + \ln(A_e)$$

title: Arrhenius rate constant for amino acid racemization — arrhenius
code: arrhenius(Ae = exp(42.12), Ea = 31.5, Temp = 278, R = 0.001987)

title: Arrhenius rate constant for amino acid racemization — arrhenius
text: Ae Pre-exponential (frequency) factor. Default exp(42.12) (Kaufman 2000). Ea Activation energy kcal/mol. Default 31.5. Temp Temperature Kelvin. Default 278 (≈ 5°C). R Gas constant kcal K\(^{-1}\) mol\(^{-1}\). Default 0.001987.

title: Arrhenius rate constant for amino acid racemization — arrhenius
text: Numeric vector rate constants \(k\) supplied temperature(s).

title: Arrhenius rate constant for amino acid racemization — arrhenius
code: # Rate constant at 5°C arrhenius(Temp = 278) #> [1] 3.362735e-07  # Rate constant across a temperature range T_K <- seq(273, 298, by = 1) plot(T_K - 273, arrhenius(Temp = T_K), type = "l",      xlab = "Temperature (°C)", ylab = "k")

what: Incremental racemization in power-law space — dRacPL
title: Incremental racemization in power-law space — dRacPL
text: Computes increment \((D/L)^x\) accumulated one timestep rate constant kt. Racemization integrated power-law \((D/L)^x\) space (Bada linearization) rather D/L space, \((D/L)^x\) increases linearly \(k_T t\) avoids sigmoidal curvature makes D/L accumulation rate-dependent current value.

title: Incremental racemization in power-law space — dRacPL
code: dRacPL(kt, delta.yr)

title: Incremental racemization in power-law space — dRacPL
text: kt Numeric. Rate constant arrhenius. delta.yr Numeric. Timestep years.

title: Incremental racemization in power-law space — dRacPL
text: Numeric vector incremental \((D/L)^x\) values.

title: Incremental racemization in power-law space — dRacPL
code: kt <- arrhenius(Temp = 278) dRacPL(kt, delta.yr = 10) #> [1] 3.362735e-06

what: Apply sediment burial temperature damping — dampSedTemps
title: Apply sediment burial temperature damping — dampSedTemps
text: sediment buried deeper, seasonal air temperature signal attenuates: summer winter sediment temperatures converge toward mean annual. function models process linear interpolation full seasonal amplitude (fresh surface sediment, weight1 = startDamp) attenuated signal (deeply buried sediment, weight1 = endDamp) dampTime ka. physical motivation thermal diffusion sediment column.

title: Apply sediment burial temperature damping — dampSedTemps
code: dampSedTemps(sumT, winT, age, startDamp = 1, endDamp = 0.5, dampTime = 8)

title: Apply sediment burial temperature damping — dampSedTemps
text: sumT Numeric vector summer half-year air temperatures (°C). winT Numeric vector winter half-year air temperatures (°C). age Numeric vector ages (ka BP), length sumT winT. Sorted old--young internally. startDamp Weight -season temperature surface (recent). Default 1 (attenuation surface). endDamp Weight -season temperature full burial. Default 0.5 (summer winter sediment temperatures equal depth). dampTime Time scale damping applied (ka). Default 8.

title: Apply sediment burial temperature damping — dampSedTemps
text: data frame columns age, sumT, winT, sumTsed (dampened summer sediment temperature), winTsed (dampened winter sediment temperature).

title: Apply sediment burial temperature damping — dampSedTemps
Default AAR kinetic parameters — default_AAR_params
text: named list Arrhenius power-law constants used defaults throughout package. Values Kaufman (2000) isoleucine epimerization yet calibrated site-specific data.

title: Default AAR kinetic parameters — default_AAR_params
code: default_AAR_params

title: Default AAR kinetic parameters — default_AAR_params
text: named list four elements: Ae Pre-exponential (frequency) factor: exp(42.12). Ea Activation energy kcal/mol: 31.5. R Gas constant kcal K\(^{-1}\) mol\(^{-1}\): 0.001987. x Power-law exponent D/L linearization: 3.

what: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
title: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
text: Computes amplitude attenuation sinusoidal surface temperature forcing burial depth depth_m, using analytical solution 1D heat equation semi-infinite medium: $$T(z,t) = T_\mathrm{mean} + \, e^{-z/\delta}   \sin\!\left(\omega t - z/\delta\right)$$ \(\delta = \sqrt{\kappa T_\mathrm{period} / \pi}\).

title: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
code: diffusion_attenuation(depth_m, kappa_m2yr, period_yr)

title: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
text: depth_m Burial depth metres. kappa_m2yr Thermal diffusivity sediment m\(^2\)/yr. Typical silty lake sediment: ~1.6 m\(^2\)/yr (5e-8 m\(^2\)/s). period_yr Period temperature forcing years. Use 1 annual cycle; use large value (e.g., 1000) Holocene-scale mean annual changes (attenuation approaches 1 typical depths).

title: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
text: Numeric vector attenuation factors (0 1).

title: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
text: kappa_m2yr = 1.6 period_yr = 1 (annual cycle), \(\delta \approx 0.71\) m. seasonal amplitude reduced 37% 0.71 m depth effectively zero ~2-3 m. 50 cm/ka sedimentation rate, seasonal signal largely attenuated samples older ~4-6 ka.

title: Thermal diffusion attenuation factor for a periodic temperature signal — diffusion_attenuation
code: depths <- seq(0, 3, by = 0.1) plot(depths,      diffusion_attenuation(depths, kappa_m2yr = 1.6, period_yr = 1),      type = "l", xlab = "Depth (m)", ylab = "Attenuation")

what: Return lake bottom water temperature at one or more times — get_temp_at_time
title: Return lake bottom water temperature at one or more times — get_temp_at_time
text: Retrieves mean annual lake bottom water temperature (°C) requested time(s). Accepts either constant scalar time-varying data frame.

title: Return lake bottom water temperature at one or more times — get_temp_at_time
code: get_temp_at_time(t_ka, temp_input)

title: Return lake bottom water temperature at one or more times — get_temp_at_time
text: t_ka Numeric vector times ka BP evaluate temperature. temp_input Temperature specification. Either: scalar single numeric value; temperature constant time. data.frame Columns age_ka (ka BP) temp_C (°C). Values linearly interpolated; flat extrapolation beyond data range.

title: Return lake bottom water temperature at one or more times — get_temp_at_time
text: Numeric vector temperatures (°C), length t_ka.

title: Return lake bottom water temperature at one or more times — get_temp_at_time
code: get_temp_at_time(c(0, 5, 10), temp_input = 5) #> [1] 5 5 5  temp_df <- data.frame(age_ka = c(0, 10), temp_C = c(5, 8)) get_temp_at_time(c(0, 5, 10), temp_input = temp_df) #> [1] 5.0 6.5 8.0

title: Log-likelihood for downcore D/L data at known temperature — log_lik_downcore

Ea Activation energy (kcal/mol). x Power-law exponent. log_sigma Log observation noise standard deviation. downcore_data Data frame sim_downcore_data. age_model Age-depth model make_age_model. temp_model Bottom water temperature (scalar data frame).

what: Log-likelihood for downcore D/L data at unknown temperature — log_lik_temperature

title: Log-likelihood for downcore D/L data at unknown temperature — log_lik_temperature
text: T_knots Numeric vector temperatures (deg C) age control points. knot_ages_ka Numeric vector ages (ka BP) control points. log_sigma Log observation noise standard deviation. kinetics_params Named vector list elements log_Ae, Ea, x. downcore_data Data frame columns depth_cm DL_obs. age_model Age-depth model.

title: Log-posterior for kinetic parameters (Stage 1) — log_posterior_kinetics
text: Combines likelihoods heating experiment data known-temperature (4deg C) calibration core kinetics prior.

title: Log-posterior for kinetic parameters (Stage 1) — log_posterior_kinetics
code: log_posterior_kinetics(   params,   heating_data,   downcore_cal_data,   age_model_cal,   temp_cal = 4 )

title: Log-posterior for kinetic parameters (Stage 1) — log_posterior_kinetics
text: params Named numeric vector: log_Ae, Ea, x, log_sigma. heating_data Data frame sim_heating_data. downcore_cal_data Data frame sim_downcore_data 4deg C calibration lake. age_model_cal Age-depth model calibration core. temp_cal Bottom water temperature calibration lake (default 4deg C).

title: Log-posterior for kinetic parameters (Stage 1) — log_posterior_kinetics
text: Scalar log-posterior (possibly -Inf invalid parameters).

title: Log-posterior for temperature history (Stage 2) — log_posterior_temperature
text: Kinetic parameters fixed values kinetics_params (typically Stage 1 posterior means).

title: Log-posterior for temperature history (Stage 2) — log_posterior_temperature
code: log_posterior_temperature(   params,   knot_ages_ka,   kinetics_params,   downcore_target_data,   age_model_target )

title: Log-posterior for temperature history (Stage 2) — log_posterior_temperature
text: params Named numeric vector temperature knot values log_sigma. Names: T_0ka, T_2ka, ..., log_sigma. knot_ages_ka Numeric vector knot ages (ka BP). kinetics_params Named vector list log_Ae, Ea, x. downcore_target_data Data frame sim_downcore_data unknown-temperature target core. age_model_target Age-depth model target core.

title: Log-posterior for temperature history (Stage 2) — log_posterior_temperature
text: Scalar log-posterior.

title: Log-prior for kinetic parameters — log_prior_kinetics
text: Weakly informative priors centred Kaufman (2000) literature values. Parameters sampled transformed scales (log_Ae, log_sigma) enforce positivity without hard truncation.

title: Log-prior for kinetic parameters — log_prior_kinetics
code: log_prior_kinetics(log_Ae, Ea, x, log_sigma)

title: Log-prior for kinetic parameters — log_prior_kinetics
text: log_Ae Log pre-exponential factor. Prior: Normal(42.12, 2). Ea Activation energy (kcal/mol). Prior: Normal(31.5, 5). x Power-law exponent. Prior: Normal(3, 0.5). log_sigma Log observation noise SD. Prior: Normal(log(0.02), 1).

title: Log-prior for kinetic parameters — log_prior_kinetics
text: Scalar log-prior.

what: Log-prior for temperature history control points — log_prior_temperature
title: Log-prior for temperature history control points — log_prior_temperature
text: Weakly informative Normal(5, 3) prior knot temperature, plus first-difference smoothness penalty discourages large jumps adjacent control points.

title: Log-prior for temperature history control points — log_prior_temperature
code: log_prior_temperature(T_knots, log_sigma)

title: Log-prior for temperature history control points — log_prior_temperature
text: T_knots Numeric vector temperatures age control points (deg C). log_sigma Log observation noise SD. Prior: Normal(log(0.02), 1).

title: Log-prior for temperature history control points — log_prior_temperature
text: Scalar log-prior.

what: Build an age-depth model from tie-point depths and ages — make_age_model
title: Build an age-depth model from tie-point depths and ages — make_age_model
text: Creates pair linear-interpolation functions converting depth (cm) age (ka BP) directions. functions use flat extrapolation (rule = 2) beyond range control points.

title: Build an age-depth model from tie-point depths and ages — make_age_model
code: make_age_model(depth_cm, age_ka)

title: Build an age-depth model from tie-point depths and ages — make_age_model
text: depth_cm Numeric vector tie-point depths cm. Must start 0 (sediment surface) monotonically increasing. age_ka Numeric vector ages ka BP corresponding depth_cm. Must start 0 length depth_cm.

title: Build an age-depth model from tie-point depths and ages — make_age_model
text: named list two elements: depth_to_age function f(depth_cm) returns age (ka BP). age_to_depth function f(age_ka) returns depth (cm).

title: Build an age-depth model from tie-point depths and ages — make_age_model
code: am <- make_age_model(depth_cm = c(0, 100, 300, 500),                      age_ka   = c(0,   2,   6,  10)) am$depth_to_age(250)  # age at 250 cm #> [1] 5 am$age_to_depth(5)    # depth at 5 ka BP #> [1] 250

code: posterior_predictive_DL(   mcmc_out,   age_model,   depths_cm,   temp_model,   AAR_params_fn = function(row) {      list(Ae = exp(row[["log_Ae"]]), Ea = row[["Ea"]],     R = 0.001987, x = row[["x"]])  },   observed_data = NULL,   burnin = 1000,   n_draws = 200 )

text: mcmc_out List returned run_mcmc. age_model Age-depth model. depths_cm Numeric vector depths evaluate predictions. temp_model Temperature model predictions. AAR_params_fn Function taking one row posterior sample matrix returning AAR_params list. Defaults extracting log_Ae, Ea, x Stage 1 posterior. observed_data Optional data frame columns depth_cm DL_obs overlay points. burnin Integer. Burn-iterations discard. Default 1000. n_draws Integer. Number posterior draws. Default 200.

what: Predict D/L for lab heating experiments — predict_heating_DL
title: Predict D/L for lab heating experiments — predict_heating_DL
text: Forward model constant-temperature, constant-time heating experiments. Assumes starting D/L = 0 (surface sediment negligible natural racemization). Reuses arrhenius dRacPL.

title: Predict D/L for lab heating experiments — predict_heating_DL
code: predict_heating_DL(temp_C, time_yr, AAR_params)

title: Predict D/L for lab heating experiments — predict_heating_DL
text: temp_C Numeric vector heating temperatures °C. time_yr Numeric vector heating durations years. AAR_params Named list elements Ae, Ea, R, x (see default_AAR_params).

text: Numeric vector predicted D/L values.

title: Predict D/L for lab heating experiments — predict_heating_DL

what: Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history — racemize
title: Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history — racemize
text: Integrates racemization oldest youngest point temperature time series, splitting timestep summer winter halves. Returns final D/L ratio back-transforming \((D/L)^x\) space.

title: Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history — racemize
code: racemize(age, sumT, winT, x, dT, startingDL = 0, scenario = NA, ...)

title: Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history — racemize
text: age Numeric vector ages (ka BP). Sorted old--young internally. sumT Numeric vector summer half-year temperatures (°C), length age. winT Numeric vector winter half-year temperatures (°C), length age. x Power-law exponent. Default 3. dT Timestep years. startingDL Starting D/L value. Default 0 (unracemized). scenario Character label stored output. Default NA. ... Additional arguments passed arrhenius.

title: Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history — racemize
text: one-row data frame columns age (maximum input age), DL, scenario.

title: Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history — racemize
code: age  <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.01) sumT <- rep(15, length(age)) winT <- rep(4,  length(age)) racemize(age, sumT, winT, x = 3, dT = 10) #>   age        DL scenario #> 1  10 0.2383851       NA

what: Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping) — racemizeSample
title: Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping) — racemizeSample
text: Full pipeline one sample: selects portion temperature history deposition present, applies dampSedTemps attenuate seasonal signal burial depth, integrates racemization via racemize.

title: Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping) — racemizeSample
code: racemizeSample(   sampleAge,   ageVec,   sumT,   winT,   x,   dT,   startingDL = 0,   scenario = NA,   startDamp = 1,   endDamp = 0.5,   dampTime = 10,   ... )

title: Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping) — racemizeSample
text: sampleAge Age sample (ka BP). temperature history older age used. ageVec Numeric vector ages spanning full temperature history (ka BP). sumT Numeric vector summer half-year temperatures (°C), length ageVec. winT Numeric vector winter half-year temperatures (°C), length ageVec. x Power-law exponent. dT Timestep years. startingDL Starting D/L value. Default 0. scenario Character label output. Default NA. startDamp, endDamp, dampTime Passed dampSedTemps. ... Additional arguments passed arrhenius.

title: Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping) — racemizeSample
text: one-row data frame columns age, DL, scenario.

title: Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping) — racemizeSample
code: age  <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.01) sumT <- rep(15, length(age)) winT <- rep(4,  length(age)) racemizeSample(sampleAge = 5, ageVec = age, sumT = sumT, winT = winT,                x = 3, dT = 10) #>   age        DL scenario #> 1   5 0.1780704       NA

what: Simulate AAR racemization for a depth series of sediment samples — racemize_depth_series
title: Simulate AAR racemization for a depth series of sediment samples — racemize_depth_series
text: Applies racemize_one_depth vector sample depths returns results single data frame. primary entry point forward-modelling downcore D/L profile.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a depth series of sediment samples — racemize_depth_series
code: racemize_depth_series(   depths_cm,   age_model,   temp_model,   sumT_model = NULL,   winT_model = NULL,   dT_yr = 10,   AAR_params = default_AAR_params,   kappa_m2yr = 1.6 )

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a depth series of sediment samples — racemize_depth_series
text: depths_cm Numeric vector sample depths cm. age_model Age-depth model make_age_model rate_to_age_model. temp_model Mean annual lake bottom water temperature: scalar data.frame(age_ka, temp_C). sumT_model Optional summer half-year temperature (format temp_model). Supply winT_model seasonal mode. winT_model Optional winter half-year temperature. dT_yr Timestep years. Default 10. AAR_params Named list kinetic parameters. Default default_AAR_params. kappa_m2yr Sediment thermal diffusivity m\(^2\)/yr. Default 1.6.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a depth series of sediment samples — racemize_depth_series
text: data frame columns depth_cm, deposition_age_ka, DL.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a depth series of sediment samples — racemize_depth_series
code: am     <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500) depths <- seq(0, 500, by = 10) out    <- racemize_depth_series(depths, age_model = am, temp_model = 5) head(out) #>   depth_cm deposition_age_ka         DL #> 1        0               0.0 0.00000000 #> 2       10               0.2 0.04066688 #> 3       20               0.4 0.05123706 #> 4       30               0.6 0.05865179 #> 5       40               0.8 0.06455465 #> 6       50               1.0 0.06953939

what: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
title: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
text: Computes expected D/L ratio sediment sample currently depth depth_cm integrating racemization moment deposition present. timestep, sample's burial depth computed age-depth model, sediment temperature derived lake bottom water temperature optional thermal diffusion attenuation seasonal signal.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
code: racemize_one_depth(   depth_cm,   age_model,   temp_model,   sumT_model = NULL,   winT_model = NULL,   dT_yr = 10,   AAR_params = default_AAR_params,   kappa_m2yr = 1.6 )

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
text: depth_cm Current depth sample cm. age_model Age-depth model make_age_model rate_to_age_model. temp_model Mean annual lake bottom water temperature: scalar (°C) data.frame(age_ka, temp_C). sumT_model Optional summer half-year lake bottom temperature (format temp_model). Supply winT_model activate seasonal mode. winT_model Optional winter half-year lake bottom temperature. Supply sumT_model activate seasonal mode. dT_yr Timestep years. Default 10. AAR_params Named list kinetic parameters. Default default_AAR_params. kappa_m2yr Sediment thermal diffusivity m\(^2\)/yr. Used seasonal mode attenuate seasonal anomaly depth. Default 1.6.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
text: one-row data frame columns depth_cm, deposition_age_ka, DL.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
text: Mean-annual mode (default): sediment temperature equals lake bottom water temperature depths. Valid e-folding depth Holocene-period signals (centuries millennia) much larger typical core depths. Seasonal mode (sumT_model winT_model supplied): seasonal anomaly attenuated burial depth via diffusion_attenuation period_yr = 1. timestep split equal summer winter halves. Arrhenius relationship nonlinear, identical mean temperatures different seasonal amplitudes produce different D/L values (Jensen's inequality). seasonal signal largely gone ~2-3 m depth.

title: Simulate AAR racemization for a single sediment sample at a given depth — racemize_one_depth
code: am <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500) racemize_one_depth(depth_cm = 250, age_model = am, temp_model = 5) #>   depth_cm deposition_age_ka        DL #> 1      250                 5 0.1189107  # Seasonal mode racemize_one_depth(depth_cm = 250, age_model = am,                    temp_model = 5, sumT_model = 10, winT_model = 0) #>   depth_cm deposition_age_ka        DL #> 1      250                 5 0.1218346

what: Build an age-depth model from a constant sedimentation rate — rate_to_age_model
title: Build an age-depth model from a constant sedimentation rate — rate_to_age_model
text: Convenience wrapper around make_age_model common case constant sedimentation rate. Constructs two-point age-depth model anchored surface (0 cm, 0 ka) max_depth_cm.

title: Build an age-depth model from a constant sedimentation rate — rate_to_age_model
code: rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka, max_depth_cm)

title: Build an age-depth model from a constant sedimentation rate — rate_to_age_model
text: rate_cm_per_ka Sedimentation rate cm/ka. max_depth_cm Maximum depth (cm) core.

title: Build an age-depth model from a constant sedimentation rate — rate_to_age_model
text: named list depth_to_age age_to_depth functions (see make_age_model).

title: Build an age-depth model from a constant sedimentation rate — rate_to_age_model
code: am <- rate_to_age_model(rate_cm_per_ka = 50, max_depth_cm = 500) am$depth_to_age(250)  # should return 5 ka #> [1] 5

title: Random-walk Metropolis-Hastings MCMC sampler — run_mcmc
text: simple general-purpose sampler. iteration proposes new parameter vector adding independent Gaussian noise current state, accepts rejects via Metropolis criterion.

title: Random-walk Metropolis-Hastings MCMC sampler — run_mcmc
code: run_mcmc(log_post_fn, init, n_iter, proposal_sd, ...)

title: Random-walk Metropolis-Hastings MCMC sampler — run_mcmc
text: log_post_fn Function taking named numeric vector returning scalar log-posterior. init Named numeric vector starting parameter values. n_iter Integer. Total iterations (including burn-). proposal_sd Named numeric vector proposal standard deviations, one per parameter. Tune 20-40% acceptance rate. ... Additional arguments passed log_post_fn.

title: Random-walk Metropolis-Hastings MCMC sampler — run_mcmc
text: list : samples Matrix [n_iter x n_params] column names init. acceptance_rate Overall Metropolis acceptance rate. log_post Numeric vector log-posterior values iteration.

title: Random-walk Metropolis-Hastings MCMC sampler — run_mcmc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # See vignette("kinetics-fitting") for a worked example. } # }

what: Simulate downcore D/L observations from a known-temperature lake — sim_downcore_data
title: Simulate downcore D/L observations from a known-temperature lake — sim_downcore_data
text: Generates synthetic downcore D/L values running forward model (racemize_depth_series) adding Gaussian noise. Used 4°C calibration core (Stage 1) unknown-temperature target core (Stage 2).

title: Simulate downcore D/L observations from a known-temperature lake — sim_downcore_data
code: sim_downcore_data(   age_model,   temp_model,   sample_ages_ka,   AAR_params = default_AAR_params,   sigma = 0.015,   seed = NULL,   sumT_model = NULL,   winT_model = NULL )

title: Simulate downcore D/L observations from a known-temperature lake — sim_downcore_data
text: age_model Age-depth model make_age_model rate_to_age_model. temp_model Bottom water temperature history: scalar data.frame(age_ka, temp_C). sample_ages_ka Numeric vector sample ages (ka BP). Converted depths via age model. AAR_params True kinetic parameters used generate data. Default default_AAR_params. sigma Standard deviation Gaussian observation noise. Default 0.015. seed Optional integer seed. sumT_model Optional summer half-year temperature. winT_model Optional winter half-year temperature.

title: Simulate downcore D/L observations from a known-temperature lake — sim_downcore_data
text: data frame columns: depth_cm, deposition_age_ka, age_ka, DL_pred, DL_obs.

title: Simulate downcore D/L observations from a known-temperature lake — sim_downcore_data

what: Simulate lab heating experiment D/L data — sim_heating_data
title: Simulate lab heating experiment D/L data — sim_heating_data
text: Generates synthetic D/L observations sediment heated constant elevated temperatures, following controlled-temperature experiment design NSF proposal 2317409 (80, 100, 110°C; 8 time steps; 3 replicates per condition, ~72 total observations).

title: Simulate lab heating experiment D/L data — sim_heating_data
code: sim_heating_data(   temps_C = c(80, 100, 110),   times_yr = c(1/8760, 1/365, 7/365, 30/365, 90/365, 180/365, 270/365, 1),   n_reps = 3,   AAR_params = default_AAR_params,   sigma = 0.01,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate lab heating experiment D/L data — sim_heating_data
text: temps_C Numeric vector heating temperatures (°C). Default c(80, 100, 110). times_yr Numeric vector heating durations (years). Default covers ~1 hour 1 year: c(1/8760, 1/365, 7/365, 30/365, 90/365, 180/365, 270/365, 1). n_reps Integer. Replicates per temperature-time combination. Default 3. AAR_params Named list true kinetic parameters used generate data. Default default_AAR_params. sigma Standard deviation Gaussian observation noise added predicted D/L. Default 0.01. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility.

title: Simulate lab heating experiment D/L data — sim_heating_data
text: data frame columns: temp_C, time_yr, replicate, DL_pred, DL_obs.

title: Simulate lab heating experiment D/L data — sim_heating_data
